title: quercus pedunculata var. brevipes
intro: Quercus pedunculata var. brevipes is a variety of the pedunculate oak tree, a deciduous species native to parts of Europe. The bark and leaves of this plant are used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus pedunculata var. brevipes include reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant may be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
